title: fix(middleware): subscribe middleware before initializing main index
body:
## Summary

This PR changes the implementation of `instantSearchInstance.start()` so that it subscribes middlewares **BEFORE** `mainIndex.init()` (It was the opposite.)

### What it solves

The router middleware used to apply the router state into `_initialUiState` at its creation, not in `subscribe()`. It was problematic when `instantSearchInstance` is re-used and have different router state that is not being taken into account. So this change allows the router middleware to modify initialUiState at `subscribe()`.

This PR also updates the implementation of insights middleware. It used to rely on the helper to set and get user token. Now at `subscribe()`, the main index is not initialized, which means there is no helper yet. So it now adds a dummy configure widget to apply `clickAnalytics` and `userToken`.



---

<details>
<summary>previous description</summary>
This PR fixes the router middleware to set correct initial ui state when instantSearchInstance is re-used.

### When does this happen?

In Vue InstantSearch, when `ais-instant-search` component gets unmounted, it calls `dispose()`.
https://github.com/algolia/vue-instantsearch/blob/2d6879be7a16bbf7fc6465891180304782d492e7/src/util/createInstantSearchComponent.js#L72:L74

Later when the component gets mounted again, it will call `instantSearchInstance.start()`.

### Why is it broken?

https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/blob/0e151a9552092807ecbc6993f3f6193fef621f44/src/middlewares/createRouterMiddleware.ts#L60:L63

Inside `createRouterMiddleware`, we read the url from the router at creation of router middleware, whereas it was supposed to be at `subscribe()`. Thus, it reads only once, and when it's re-used it will stay outdated.

---
**More info:**

When the router middleware is created, it updates `instantSearchInstance._initialUiState`. At this point, `instantSearchInstance.start()` hasn't been called yet. When `start()` is called, it initialize the mainIndex with `mainIndex.init(...)` and even though corresponding widgets don't exist in the widget tree, it takes initialUiState into account and keep it for a while.

```js
const search = instantsearch({
   indexName: 'hello',
   searchClient,
   initialUiState: {
    hello: {
      query: 'world'
    }
   }
})

search.start()
```

Even though there is no `searchBox` widget to hold `query` inside the uiState, it will hold `query` for a while.

* If `searchBox` is added, then it will trigger a new search with the query (Before then, the search param doesn't include it.)
* Before `searchBox` is added, if `search.getUiState()` is called, it will [re-evaluate it](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/blob/0e151a9552092807ecbc6993f3f6193fef621f44/src/lib/InstantSearch.ts#L612) and `query` disappears.

This seems inconsistent, considering we normally eliminate states from uiState if there is no corresponding widget (no matter we like the behavior or not.)

---

### Solutions

#### Fix 1. Use `setUiState` instead of updating initialUiState.

Instead of manipulating the private instance variable `_initialUiState`, leveraging the public API `setUiState` seems solid. However, this unnecessarily triggers uiState change to `instantsearch({ onStateChange })` and to middlewares. It also could cause one more search API calls (empty one + another one with the new state.) If we call setUiState from the router middleware when only there is something to set, then it's less problematic.

Another problem with this approach is, if corresponding widgets are not in the widget tree, the result of `setUiState` will go away, whereas what's written in `initialUiState` remains regardless.

#### Fix 2. Call `middleware.subscribe()` before `mainIndex.init()`

Then it can get to update `_initialUiState` before it's passed to `mainIndex.init()`, but this doesn't seem right. It's better to have middlewares started after we initialize the main index and settle down with states. Also, this change will break the insights middleware and possibly custom middlewares.

#### Fix 3. Re-create middlewares when restarted 👈

When the instance gets reused and restarted, we'd probably want to re-create the middlewares. The definition of middleware in IS.js is "a function that returns an object." We could re-run the functions to replace the middleware objects. This is a non-breaking change, and possibly will work. I will try it out and will keep this PR updated.
</details>
